perturbation.original_text: 3.1. Invoices and Payment. FNW will send Sponsor invoices Sponsorship Fee in -------------------- two (2) monthly installments beginning September 1, 1999. Invoices are payable thirty (30) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1. Invoices and Payment. FNW will send Sponsor invoices Sponsorship Fee in -------------------- two (2) monthly installments beginning September 1, 1999. Invoices are payable upon receipt. Late fees and interest are at the discretion of FNW.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified a 30-day payment window and detailed late fee/interest structure. The modified text removes the 30-day grace period, making invoices payable immediately and gives the FNW full discretion over late fees and interest which contradicts Washington state laws regarding reasonable late fee charges.
perturbation.law_explanation: RCW 19.86.020 declares unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce unlawful. By removing the clearly defined payment terms and grace period, the agreement becomes unfairly weighted in favor of FNW, potentially enabling predatory fee structures and practices violating consumer protections against deceptive billing practices.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 19.86.020: Unfair competition, practices, declared unlawful. Print RCWs > Title 19 > Chapter 19.86 > Section 19.86.020 19.86.010 << 19.86.020 >> 19.86.023 PDF RCW 19.86.020 Unfair competition, practices, declared unlawful. Unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful. [ 1961 c 216 s 2 .] NOTES: Hearing instrument dispensing, advertising, etc. — Application: RCW 18.35.180 .

perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the original 30-day payment window and the defined late fee/interest structure. The new text makes invoices payable immediately and gives FNW full discretion over late fees and interest. This contradicts RCW 19.86.020, which declares unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in trade or commerce unlawful. By removing the defined payment terms, the agreement becomes unfairly weighted in favor of FNW, potentially enabling predatory fee structures, violating consumer protections against deceptive billing practices. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 both state: 'Unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful.' The removal of the payment terms constitutes a practice that could be deemed unfair or deceptive.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Arbitration. Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this ----------- Agreement, including, but not limited to a claim based on or arising from an alleged tort will, at the request of any party be determined by arbitration in accordance with the Federal Arbitration Act (9 U.S.C. Section 1, et seq.) under the auspices and rules of the American Arbitration Association ("AAA"). The AAA will be instructed by either or both parties to prepare a list of judges who have retired from the Superior Court of the State of Washington, a higher Washington court or any federal court. 'Within 10 days of receipt of this list, each party may strike one name from the list. The AAA will then appoint an arbitrator from the name(s) remaining on the list. The arbitration will be conducted from Seattle, Washington. Any controversy in interpretation or enforcement of this provision or whether a dispute is arbitrable, will be determined by the arbitrators. Judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrator(s) may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief or in pursuit of an ancillary remedy, does not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Arbitration. Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this ----------- Agreement, including, but not limited to a claim based on or arising from an alleged tort will be determined by arbitration. Judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrator(s) may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief or in pursuit of an ancillary remedy, does not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: By removing references to the Federal Arbitration Act and the American Arbitration Association, the contract fails to establish a clear framework for arbitration proceedings. The contract no longer specifies the rules or procedures that will govern the arbitration, making it impossible to tell which arbitration rules will be used, potentially violating requirements for consumer arbitration agreements to be clear and definite.
perturbation.law_explanation: RCW 7.04A.090 relates to the initiation of arbitration and requires reasonable notice, which includes the method agreed upon by the parties. By removing reference to the AAA and the FAA, there's no clear agreement, potentially creating issues when initiating an arbitration, thereby creating a legal contradiction and violating the Uniform Arbitration Act.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 7.04A.090: Initiation of arbitration. Print RCWs > Title 7 > Chapter 7.04A > Section 7.04A.090 7.04A.080 << 7.04A.090 >> 7.04A.100 PDF RCW 7.04A.090 Initiation of arbitration. (1) A person initiates an arbitration proceeding by giving notice in a record to the other parties to the agreement to arbitrate in the agreed manner between the parties or, in the absence of agreement, by mail certified or registered, return receipt requested and obtained, or by service as authorized for the initiation of a civil action. The notice must describe the nature of the controversy and the remedy sought. (2) Unless a person interposes an objection as to lack or insufficiency of notice under RCW 7.04A.150 (3) not later than the commencement of the arbitration hearing, the person's appearance at the hearing waives any objection to lack of or insufficiency of notice. (3) A claim sought to be arbitrated is subject to the same limitations of time for the commencement of actions as if the claim had been asserted in a court. [ 2013 c 92 s 1 ; 2005 c 433 s 9 .]

perturbation.justification: The modified contract removes key references to the Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) and the American Arbitration Association (AAA), which are essential for establishing a clear framework for arbitration. This omission contradicts RCW 7.04A.090, which requires that arbitration be initiated in the 'agreed manner between the parties.' Without specifying the AAA rules or the FAA, the modified contract fails to establish an agreed-upon method, potentially leading to disputes about the arbitration process and violating the Uniform Arbitration Act. The scraped snippets of RCW 7.04A.090 highlight the importance of adhering to the agreed manner for initiating arbitration, which is absent in the changed text.
